refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall ensure teleoperation communication latency p95 ≤ 150 ms end-to-end to enable safe remote operation.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is decomposed to explicitly specify the latency percentile and end-to-end scope, focusing on measurable performance for safety-critical teleoperation.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the 95th percentile latency of the teleoperation communication path under agreed network conditions using telemetry data and network monitoring tools.
sub_frs: FR1: Minimize communication latency in the teleoperation data path.
FR2: Ensure reliable and consistent network link performance to support latency requirements.
FR3: Provide real-time telemetry feedback to monitor latency and system status.

candidate_dps: DP1: Optimize data encoding and compression algorithms to reduce payload size.
DP2: Use dedicated low-latency network protocols and QoS prioritization.
DP3: Implement edge computing modules to process data closer to the operator and vehicle.
DP4: Design telemetry feedback loops with timestamp synchronization for latency measurement.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP2: Use dedicated low-latency network protocols and QoS prioritization.

potential_sources: Interaction between network protocol optimization and telemetry feedback timing.
Dependency on vehicle communication hardware capabilities.
coupling_check.notes: DP2 logically isolates latency control to network protocol and QoS layers, minimizing coupling with other DPs.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network infrastructure supports QoS and low-latency protocols.
Risk of network variability impacting latency despite protocol optimizations.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider integrating edge computing (DP3) if network conditions degrade beyond acceptable latency.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for managing teleoperation communication protocols, latency optimization, and QoS enforcement.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Physical and logical network connection enabling data exchange between operator and vehicle.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Provides real-time latency and system status feedback to monitoring modules.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives teleoperation commands from operator control systems.

module_containment_scores.notes: High containment of latency-related functions; low diffusion as latency logic is centralized.
Vehicle hardware impacts latency but does not contain protocol logic.
Safety services consume latency data but do not implement latency control.
System-level coordination but less direct containment of latency control.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Latency control logic and protocol optimization are best contained within the Teleoperation module to minimize diffusion and enable focused ownership.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on Vehicle hardware capabilities may require cross-module coordination.
Network Link variability outside module control could impact latency performance.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Establish clear interface contracts with Vehicle and Network Link modules to manage dependencies and monitor latency impact.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DP (network protocol and QoS prioritization) directly addresses the refined FR of minimizing teleoperation latency with clear verification paths.

diffusion_risks: Some latency impact from Vehicle hardware and network variability may diffuse responsibility beyond Teleoperation module.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Latency control logic is well contained in Teleoperation, minimizing diffusion and enabling clear ownership.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Latency can be measured end-to-end via Telemetry interface, enabling verification of FR through IM.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of latency control to Teleoperation module with defined interfaces to Vehicle and Network Link.

recommended_revisions: Define explicit interface contracts with Vehicle and Network Link modules to manage latency dependencies.
Consider fallback or mitigation strategies if network conditions degrade beyond protocol optimization capabilities.

decision.justification: While the decomposition and assignments are sound, risks related to network variability and cross-module dependencies require refinement in interface definitions and fallback strategies.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Minimize communication latency in the teleoperation data path.
FR2: Ensure reliable and consistent network link performance to support latency requirements.
FR3: Provide real-time telemetry feedback to monitor latency and system status.

revise.why: To refine interface contracts and ownership boundaries addressing cross-module latency dependencies and risk mitigation.

assumptions: Network infrastructure supports QoS and low-latency protocols.
Vehicle hardware can support required communication performance.
open_risks: Network variability impacting latency despite protocol optimizations.
Cross-module coordination challenges between Teleoperation and Vehicle modules.
evidence_needed: Latency performance data under representative network conditions.
Verification of interface contracts and fallback mechanisms.
